* APIs that are free or have a free tier.
A .zip containing the app folder (manifest.json + app.py, or data.json for a channel app). Functional apps run Python on this host — only upload apps you trust.
A fun day, every day (National Donut Day...)
A new display starts from this one's global settings — location, language and API keys are copied so you don't retype them. They become its own from then on, and are stored on its own gateway.
Add any mix of teams and whole leagues — the app rotates through their games.
Also uses global settings: %s — set these under Global settings.
Apps watch for events and briefly interrupt the display when something happens.
Birthdays and holidays are all-day events. Turn this on if you only want things that happen at a time.
Blank uses your Location's currency (then Language).
Cities (as many as the wall has rows)
Comments (blank line separates slides, up to 3 lines each)
Couldn't get your location — check the browser's location permission.
Default timezone for clocks and time-based apps.
Delete "%s"? This removes the uploaded app for good.
Drive several gateways from one companion. Each has its own geometry, apps, playlists and settings.

Each column is an hour. The bar's HEIGHT is how warm it gets compared with the rest of the window; its COLOUR is the actual temperature — violet, blue, green, yellow, orange, red. Uses the global Location.
Edit shared settings apps rely on (API keys, location, timezone…)
Find yours at tidesandcurrents.noaa.gov (default is Boston, MA).
Geocoded to place you — used unless a precise location is set below.
Home every module — returns the whole display to its blank home position and stops anything playing
How far into the future to look. Nothing beyond this is shown.
How long each app page shows before advancing, unless an app overrides it.
ISO country code, e.g. US, GB, DE, FR, CA. Blank follows your Location (then Language).
If all countdown slots are off, Slot 1 will be used.
Image URL (leave blank for a colour demo)
Key generated and stored with your settings. Pin your own with COMPANION_VESTABOARD_KEY.
Leave blank to use your global location from the main settings.
Live — frames are sent to the display. Turn on to test apps without touching the wall.
Manually sync this companion's settings with the gateway (needs Gateway 3.1+).
Matrix Gateway walls only — physical flaps can't keep up with seconds.
Multilingual — adapts to the global Language
No global settings yet — install apps that use shared settings (weather, stocks, …).
No new gateways found — a gateway must be powered and on this network.
Off. Turn on to accept Vestaboard Local API calls (Home Assistant, scripts) at /local-api/message — this display then answers like a Vestaboard.
Off. Turn on to let an LLM client (Claude, an agent) drive the display as tools at /mcp — show a message, run an app, read the board.
Open-Meteo is keyless. OpenWeather, WeatherAPI.com, and QWeather require API keys.
Open-Meteo is keyless; the others use the Weather API key below.
OpenSky Client ID (optional; leave blank for free API)
OpenSky Client Secret (optional; higher limits with free account)
OpenWeather in this app currently supports weather + AQI only.
Optional for Open-Meteo. Required for OpenWeather, WeatherAPI.com, and QWeather.
Override the global Language for this app only.
Override the global Location for this app only (place search).
Pull grid geometry + MQTT settings from the gateway now.
QWeather in this app currently supports weather + AQI only.
Remove all per-entry overrides (follow the app's settings)
Remove this display? Its settings, playlists and triggers are kept.
Required for OpenWeather, WeatherAPI.com and QWeather; Open-Meteo needs none.
Restart the add-on to re-point this display
Search a place for exact coordinates; overrides the ZIP/city above.
Seconds each countdown is shown (before rotating to the next)
Sequence apps and composed messages, each shown for its own duration.
Set a polling rate to estimate API usage.
Shared by the YouTube subscriber, comments and livestream apps.

Show everything in capitals, even on a display that can render lowercase (a Matrix Portal). A real split-flap has no lowercase flaps and is always in capitals regardless — this is for when you prefer that look.
Show up/down and status as text only, without the colored tiles.
Simulating — nothing is sent to the physical display (the live preview still updates).
Skip price updates overnight and on weekends, when the exchange is shut.

The display language for apps that support it (look for the 🌐 badge): it translates their words and sets date order, number format and 12h/24h. Currency and holidays follow your Location, not this. Any app can override it in its own settings. Only Windows-1252 (Western-European) languages are listed — the modules can't show others.
The display that anything not naming one drives — Home Assistant, the Vestaboard API, an MCP call
The scan failed — you can still add a gateway by URL above.

The secret address of your calendar's iCal feed — in Google Calendar: Settings → your calendar → Integrate calendar → 'Secret address in iCal format'. Several calendars? Separate them with commas, and their events are merged into one timeline. Anyone holding one of these URLs can read that calendar, so they are stored and shown like a password.
These apply to this playlist entry only. Unchanged fields follow the app's own settings.
This returns the whole display to its blank home position and stops anything currently playing.
Timezones (as many as the wall has rows)
Token generated and stored with your settings. Pin your own with COMPANION_MCP_TOKEN.
Used by this app (auto-detected — not in the app's manifest)
Where it's coming from and going to. Needs a keyed provider — OpenSky's free feed has no route.
click a cell to type · arrows to move · a swatch paints the cell
